assessmentName: Azure Managed Disk Snapshot Production Optimization

principles: Discover snapshot properties, source lineage, access settings, cost, prices, and retention signals dynamically before applying rules.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for snapshot storage, redundancy, and region price comparison.
Treat snapshots primarily as storage and recovery artifacts, not performance resources.

Prefer the best valid production target: delete expired snapshots, convert process to incremental snapshots, adjust redundancy, restrict export access, improve encryption, tag ownership, or keep when retention requires it.
Never recommend deletion or redundancy reduction when recovery, compliance, legal hold, migration, audit, or restore requirements are unknown.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect snapshot properties, cost, price, lineage, and security posture with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per snapshot.
Do not fetch snapshot metrics by default; snapshots normally do not provide useful performance metrics for optimization.
Cache metric-definition absence or availability by resource type and namespace so the engine does not repeatedly probe metrics for snapshots.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per snapshot.
Use Retail Prices once per region and snapshot meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all snapshots in that region.
Use snapshot list properties, tags, age, incremental flag, source resource id, SKU, disk size, completion percent, and access settings for the first-pass recommendation.
Fetch source disk, backup vault, activity logs, restore history, locks, or policy resources only for snapshots that are expensive, old, untagged, publicly exportable, or deletion candidates.

derivedSignals.hasOwnerTag: tags.owner or tags.Owner or tags.applicationOwner exists
derivedSignals.hasExpiryTag: tags.expiresOn or tags.expiryDate or tags.retentionUntil exists
derivedSignals.publicExportAllowed: properties.publicNetworkAccess != 'Disabled' OR properties.networkAccessPolicy allows public access
derivedSignals.usesCustomerManagedKey: properties.encryption.diskEncryptionSetId exists OR encryption type indicates customer-managed key

requiredProductionFilters: delete recommendation must verify retention, owner, backup, migration, legal hold, and recent restore/export signals
target redundancy must satisfy recovery and compliance requirements
target access policy must preserve required restore/export workflows
target encryption posture must not weaken policy requirements
snapshot cleanup must consider source disk existence and snapshot series lineage
snapshot conversion recommendation should affect future snapshot process, not mutate an existing snapshot in place unless Azure supports that operation

recommendationRules.recommendation: Delete expired snapshot after final owner/recovery validation.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Require approval or automated policy evidence before deletion in production.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: isExpiredByTag == true AND no legal hold or recovery dependency is detected

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.hasOwnerTag') neq True or get_value(resource, 'signals.hasExpiryTag') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Tag owner, workload, environment, retention, and expiry; quarantine from auto-delete until ownership is resolved.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not delete solely because tags are missing; create owner workflow first.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: snapshotAgeDays > untaggedSnapshotReviewDays AND required governance tags are missing

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.sourceDiskExists') eq False and get_value(resource, 'signals.snapshotAgeDays') gte 30

recommendationRules.recommendation: Delete snapshot if recovery is no longer required, or keep only the latest approved recovery point.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Check backup vault, migration records, legal hold, and restore history before deletion.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: sourceDiskId exists AND sourceDiskExists == false AND snapshotAgeDays exceeds retention threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.isFullSnapshot') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.snapshotSeriesCount') gte 2

recommendationRules.recommendation: Change future snapshot process to use incremental snapshots where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Recommend process change for future snapshots; existing snapshot cleanup still follows retention validation.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: isFullSnapshot == true AND recurring snapshots are detected for the same source lineage

recommendationRules.recommendation: Keep required recovery points and delete older snapshots outside retention.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use sourceLineageKey grouping and retention policy to select deletion candidates.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: snapshotSeriesCount exceeds policy retention count OR oldestSnapshotInSeriesAgeDays exceeds retention policy

recommendationRules.recommendation: Review retention need, snapshot type, redundancy, region, and source lineage; delete or reduce future snapshot footprint where safe.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Rank by monthlyActualCost and age, then apply retention and recovery filters.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: monthlyActualCost exceeds configured threshold OR snapshot cost is an outlier in the subscription

recommendationRules.recommendation: Align snapshot redundancy with recovery and compliance requirements.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not reduce redundancy for production or compliance snapshots without owner approval.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: snapshot SKU redundancy is stronger or weaker than workload recovery policy

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable public network access and use DiskAccess private endpoints for snapshot import/export workflows where possible.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Preserve approved restore/export workflows while reducing public exposure.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicExportAllowed == true AND policy requires private access

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresCustomerManagedKeys') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.usesCustomerManagedKey') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use disk encryption set with customer-managed keys for snapshots that require CMK.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate source disk and restore workflow compatibility before changing encryption posture.

recommendationRules.recommendation: Ensure snapshots used for restore preserve Trusted Launch or Confidential VM disk security requirements.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Check source disk, target restore VM requirements, and security profile compatibility.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: snapshot is from sensitive OS disk and securityProfile does not satisfy policy

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate Azure Backup or managed backup policy with defined retention, restore testing, and compliance controls.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Manual snapshots can remain for migration or short-term rollback, but production backup should have policy and restore validation.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: recurring manual snapshots are used as backup substitute for production workload

selectionAlgorithm: List snapshots for the subscription or resource group.
Group snapshots by sourceUniqueId or sourceResourceId to identify snapshot series.
Calculate age, tag completeness, expiry, incremental/full status, source lineage, access posture, and encryption posture.
Fetch actual cost from Cost Management grouped by ResourceId and meter.
Fetch retail prices for snapshot storage and redundancy by region.
Rank snapshots by cost, age, missing ownership, expired retention, public access, and orphaned source lineage.
Apply production filters for retention, legal hold, backup, migration, restore history, and owner approval.
Build candidate actions for deletion, tagging, retention cleanup, incremental process change, redundancy alignment, and security hardening.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, estimated savings, risk, and required validation.

bestRecommendation.action: delete | keep | tag | change_future_process | align_redundancy | secure | no_action

knownLimitations: Snapshot optimization is mostly property, cost, retention, and security based; performance metrics are not normally useful for snapshot recommendations.
Cost Management may attribute snapshot storage through meters that require grouping by ResourceId, product, meter, and SKU to isolate snapshot costs.
Snapshot size and billed usage can differ from source disk provisioned size, especially for incremental snapshots; prefer actual cost and usage quantity when available.
Deleting snapshots can break recovery, migration, compliance, or audit workflows; production deletion requires retention and owner validation.
Full lineage and restore-safety assessment may require tags, activity logs, backup inventories, change records, or external CMDB data not present on the snapshot resource.
Existing full snapshots cannot always be converted in place to incremental snapshots; recommendation may apply to future snapshot creation workflow.

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused

validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
